note: MLETR Art. 10/11 singularity/exclusive-control evidence checker (MLETR-CONTROL-BUILD-SPEC.md MC-1). Vector 1: clean unbroken chain. Vector 2: broken chain (unknown-party transfer). Vector 3: overlapping-control fork (same holder hands off to two parties).

vectors.name: clean_chain_reliable
vectors.description: Two sequential control-transfer events, unbroken chain, well-formed digest and singularity assertion -- reliable_evidence.

integrity_ref.article: MLETR Art. 10 -- integrity of the electronic record
integrity_ref.detail: document_digest is a well-formed sha256 reference.

singularity.detail: A named platform_identity asserts sole-authoritative-copy control (singularity_assertion=true).

chain_continuity.detail: 2 control-transfer event(s) form one unbroken chain from "Carrier-A" to "Buyer-C".

exclusive_control.article: MLETR Art. 11 -- exclusive control (no overlapping claims)
exclusive_control.detail: No two parties claim overlapping control of the record at any point in time.

output_payload.note: Educational element checklist against UNCITRAL MLETR Arts. 10-11 functional-equivalence criteria for singularity and exclusive control, computed by interval/chain math over the SUPPLIED control-assertion set only. Not a legal opinion, not a registry attestation of which copy is authoritative -- verify the platform's own reliable-system status independently.

vectors.name: broken_chain_unknown_party
vectors.description: Second event's from_holder (Shipper-X) never held recognized control -- chain is broken and the transfer is an unknown-party overlap claim.

integrity_ref.article: MLETR Art. 10 -- integrity of the electronic record
integrity_ref.detail: document_digest is a well-formed sha256 reference.

singularity.detail: A named platform_identity asserts sole-authoritative-copy control (singularity_assertion=true).

chain_continuity.detail: 1 event(s) did not extend the accepted chain of custody.

exclusive_control.article: MLETR Art. 11 -- exclusive control (no overlapping claims)

overlap_events.detail: "Shipper-X" never held recognized control in the accepted chain -- an unrecognized party asserting a transfer is itself a double-control risk.

output_payload.note: Educational element checklist against UNCITRAL MLETR Arts. 10-11 functional-equivalence criteria for singularity and exclusive control, computed by interval/chain math over the SUPPLIED control-assertion set only. Not a legal opinion, not a registry attestation of which copy is authoritative -- verify the platform's own reliable-system status independently.

vectors.name: overlapping_control_fork
vectors.description: Carrier-A hands off control to both Bank-B (t=1000) and Bank-D (t=1500) -- a fork, i.e. two parties simultaneously claiming to have received exclusive control -- the classic MLETR Art.11 double-control risk.

integrity_ref.article: MLETR Art. 10 -- integrity of the electronic record
integrity_ref.detail: document_digest is a well-formed sha256 reference.

singularity.detail: A named platform_identity asserts sole-authoritative-copy control (singularity_assertion=true).

chain_continuity.detail: 1 event(s) did not extend the accepted chain of custody.

exclusive_control.article: MLETR Art. 11 -- exclusive control (no overlapping claims)

overlap_events.detail: "Carrier-A" already relinquished control before this event's timestamp -- this transfer overlaps a window the accepted chain assigned elsewhere.

Not a legal opinion, not a registry attestation of which copy is authoritative -- verify the platform's own reliable-system status independently.
